settings.title: Primary bath, down to the studs
settings.summary: A leaking 1980s bathroom stripped to the framing and rebuilt with a freestanding tub, walk-in shower, and heated tile floor.
settings.description:
What looked like a regrout job turned out to be a slow leak that had been working on the subfloor for years. We opened the room to the studs, replaced the damaged framing, and rebuilt from the waterproofing outward.

What the demo uncovered

Two joists and a section of subfloor were too far gone to keep. We photographed everything, walked the owners through the repair the same day, and had their sign-off before the framing crew came back. Nobody discovered anything in week five.

The new layout

The new layout trades a cramped corner shower and unused garden tub for a proper walk-in shower and a freestanding soaker under the window. Heated floors, a curbless entry, and blocking in the walls for future grab bars — the invisible work that makes a bathroom last decades, not years.

Finishes stay quiet on purpose: large-format porcelain on the floor, a stacked-tile shower wall, and a double vanity with drawers sized to what actually lives in a bathroom. A dedicated exhaust fan on a humidity sensor protects all of it.

Living through it

The owners kept use of the hall bath for all six weeks, and we kept the dust where it belonged with sealed doorways and floor protection along the whole route to the front door.

Final invoice matched the signed quote, including the framing repair we priced as a change order in week one.
